channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Iran claims to have struck US bases in Jordan, Bahrain, and Kuwait
item.description: The exchange followed a new wave of US bombardments against Iran, in which at least one person was killed.

item.title: At least one Belgian has lost his life in the fires in Andalusia
item.description: In total, 12 people have died while attempting to flee the blaze near Bédar.

item.title: World Cup: England defeats Norway and advances to semifinals
item.description: Their next opponents will be Argentina, who won over Switzerland 3-1.

item.title: Ten dead in Bahamas plane crash operated by local airline
item.description: The crash happened in north Andros, the largest island in the Bahamian archipelago.

item.title: Man nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane after take-off
item.description: "Fortunately, he had not removed his seatbelt."

item.title: Belgians missing after deadly wildfires in southern Spain
item.description: At least 12 people have died, eight are injured, and 23 remain missing.

item.title: Wally Funk, oldest woman to go into space, dies aged 87
item.description: Funk flew into space with Jeff Bezos in 2021, setting a world record at the age of 82 as the oldest woman to have ever been in space.

item.title: Eight men face life in prison over foiled White House birthday attack
item.description: The men have been charged over an alleged plot to attack Donald Trump’s 80th birthday celebration at the White House.

item.title: France beat Morocco 2-0 to reach World Cup semis
item.description: The win sent Les Bleus into the semi-finals, where they will face either Spain or Belgium.

item.title: EU weighs up tougher trade restrictions on illegal Israeli settlements
item.description: The proposals are reportedly contained in an "options paper" circulated to member states.

item.title: 'The EU will always defend its member states,' says Commission after Trump threatens Spain
item.description: Speaking before a meeting of the 32 NATO leaders in Ankara, Trump sharply criticised Spain, calling it a “lost cause”.

item.title: US failure to gain control of Greenland is a 'big problem', says Trump at NATO meeting
item.description: Speaking to reporters alongside NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, the US President said he was unhappy with the alliance over “what it has done with Greenland”.

item.title: Humanitarian convoy departs from Liège to support Ebola outbreak in DRC
item.description: The aid convoy will strengthen the response to the outbreak of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

item.title: Marine Le Pen to run in French presidential election
item.description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen lost her appeal over her conviction for misusing public funds on Tuesday at the Paris Court of Appeal.

item.title: EU's new border control: Does Sweden have the solution to delays and queues?
item.description: “In Sweden, we don't use self-scanning machines, but you do it at home on the couch using the app."

item.title: UN chief calls for international rules on AI to mitigate risks
item.description: "If AI is to be powerful, it must also be regulated."

item.title: Red Devils face co-hosts in tricky World Cup last-16 tie
item.description: Belgium's Red Devils face a stern test in the World Cup as they square up against the United States in Seattle. Everything points towards a tight, nervy encounter.

item.title: Dozens of people arrested in Turkey before NATO summit
item.description: Alleged "counter-terrorism" investigations led to over 50 arrests on different Turkish locations ahead of a NATO summit in Ankara. Critics suggest preventing protests was the real goal.

item.title: Oil cartel OPEC+ to ramp up oil production
item.description: Saudi Arabia, Russia and Iraq will both produce tens of thousands more barrels of oil per day to account for global imbalances, the network announced.

item.title: China and Russia to conduct joint naval exercise
item.description: A military exercise will take place off China's coast in July 2026, in the air and maritime areas, between two key strategic partners in Russia and China.
